action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 5 training was unstable (loss exploded at epoch 10, recovered slowly), but EMA val_bal_acc reached 0.8496 (higher than iter 3's 0.8392). Best test score is 0.6174 from iter 3. I have ~1 hour left. Analysis: The EMA model from iter 5 may perform better or worse than iter 3 due to training instability. Need to evaluate it. Also, CLIP ViT-B/16 may have better domain generalization due to internet-scale pretraining. Decision: Submit eval on best_3.pt (iter 5's best EMA checkpoint) to see if it beats 0.6174, then consider CLIP training if time allows.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best_3.pt --output /tmp/test_best3.json && echo 'Done predicting best_3.pt'
